title: calamintha officinalis subsp. nepetoides
intro: Calamintha officinalis subsp. nepetoides, also known as lesser calamint or Nepeta-like calamint, is a subspecies of perennial herb in the mint family, Lamiaceae. This plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Calamintha officinalis subsp. nepetoides include reducing anxiety and stress, as well as providing relief from digestive issues and respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
